summary: Iteration 10 — Major refactor regression + new rate-limit. server.py monolith split into core.py + 5 routers (auth, quiz, payments, challenges, promo). 19/19 pytest backend tests PASS covering: auth (register/login/me/logout/profile-patch/change-password/account-delete), quiz (8 categories + premium & free questions + attempts + stats), payments (packages), challenges (admin create + mine + public get + participate), promo (admin list+CRUD+seeded codes FAMILLE2026/DECOUVERTE30 + redeem upgrades plan to premium), static mascots (8/8 served at /api/static/mascots/*.png), and the new rate-limit on /api/auth/forgot-password (first 3 calls return 200, 4th returns HTTP 429 with French detail 'Trop de tentatives' and Retry-After header). Rate-limit isolation verified: login/register unaffected after forgot-pw bucket exhausted. Frontend smoke: Landing shows 8 mascots; admin login → /app/dashboard works; /app/admin/promo lists FAMILLE2026 + DECOUVERTE30; /app/quiz/cinema loads questions; /forgot-password shows confirmation block. Full E2E reset flow: register → forgot → token from backend logs → /reset-password sets new pwd → auto-redirected to /login → login with new pwd reaches dashboard → old password rejected (401). Backend logs all 'Application startup complete' with proper seeding (admin idempotent, promos idempotent, 240 questions reloaded).
critical_code_review_comments: core.py — rate-limit bucket dict is process-local and never bounded (only timestamps inside window are kept); under high cardinality of source IPs the dict grows unboundedly. Consider periodic GC or LRU cap.

core.py — rate_limit uses request.headers['x-forwarded-for'] without validating presence/format; behind a proxy that strips it, all clients share the same key (request.client.host). Currently OK because ingress sets XFF, but worth documenting.
server.py startup hook deletes all questions every boot (db.questions.delete_many({})) and re-inserts. Fine for seed-data but loses any runtime additions if such a concept were added later.
auth.py — _send_reset_email logs the full reset_link to backend logs (line 124 [RESET] ...). Useful for testing/debugging but sensitive in production; consider gating behind a DEBUG flag.
auth.py — forgot_password now always returns email_sent key (good — fixes prior enumeration concern raised in iter9). Generic response shape is consistent across known/unknown emails.
Modular split is clean: core.py (220 lines) + 5 routers stay well under the 700-line threshold; no duplicated logic spotted across routers.
seed_data_creation: test_iteration10.py creates TEST_<uuid>@example.com users (free + premium upgraded), TEST_<code> promo (deleted), TEST_del_<uuid> user (deleted). All test data prefixed TEST_ for cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor is functionally equivalent to monolith — zero regressions. Rate-limit is in-memory & per-IP — when running pytest TestRateLimit::* repeatedly, restart backend (sudo supervisorctl restart backend) to clear _rate_buckets. Browser automation runs from a different egress IP than pytest, so they don't interfere. Note: backend [RESET] log line lowercases the email (body.email.lower() in register/forgot endpoints), so when grep-ing logs for an email created via API, search lowercase.
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues found. Refactor passes all regression checks; new rate-limit behaves correctly (3 OK → 4th 429 with Retry-After and 'Trop de tentatives' message in French); isolation per-endpoint verified.
